component.name: Wayland Scroll Factor
component.summary: Adjust touchpad scroll and pinch zoom/rotate sensitivity on Wayland

description.p: Wayland Scroll Factor provides per-user control over touchpad scroll sensitivity and pinch zoom/rotate behavior on GNOME Wayland and Hyprland.
Compatibility release for Hyprland 0.55+ Lua configurations.

ul.li: hyprland: apply live scroll factors through hyprctl eval when the Lua config manager is active
hyprland: verify the live touchpad scroll factor after runtime apply
hyprland: install an optional Lua helper for reload-safe scroll persistence
diagnostics: report whether Hyprland scroll uses keyword or lua-eval

description.p: Stable release with GNOME Wayland support and new Hyprland backend support.

ul.li: hyprland: added native live scroll tuning through hyprctl
hyprland: added gestures-only preload mode for pinch zoom and pinch rotate tuning
hyprland: added wsf-hyprland launcher shim for start-hyprland
hyprland: added wsf-session-wrapper for greeters that select or remember sessions
gnome: improved runtime reload behavior on recent Mutter/GNOME builds
config: parse numeric factors independently from the user's locale
diagnostics: report live compositor preload state and Hyprland activation hints

description.p: Testing patch release focused on runtime activation and compatibility.

ul.li: runtime: factor changes now reload live inside GNOME Shell without logout once WSF is already active
runtime: WSF removes its own preload entry from child processes to reduce inherited-preload issues with snap apps
doctor/status: added systemd user manager and GNOME Shell activation diagnostics
enable/disable: now try systemctl --user daemon-reexec automatically

description.p: Testing patch release with installer compatibility fixes.

ul.li: bootstrap: Fedora/DNF now supports both libinput-utils and libinput-tools with fallback logic
install: meson setup now passes the source directory explicitly when called from outside the repo
gui: compatibility fix for accessibility label API differences in GTK bindings
